cystitis.description: Inflammation of the bladder, usually caused by a bacterial urinary tract infection (UTI). Common in women and can cause significant discomfort.
cystitis.advice: Drink plenty of water, avoid caffeine and alcohol, and see a doctor for antibiotics if symptoms persist.

vulvodynia.description: Chronic pain or discomfort around the opening of the vagina (vulva) with no identifiable cause, lasting at least 3 months.
vulvodynia.advice: Avoid irritants, use cotton underwear, apply cool compresses, and consult a gynecologist for treatment options.

nose disorder.description: A broad category covering conditions affecting the nasal passages, including chronic rhinitis, nasal polyps, and deviated septum.
nose disorder.advice: Use saline nasal spray, avoid allergens, and consult an ENT specialist if symptoms are chronic.

complex regional pain syndrome.description: A chronic pain condition that typically affects an arm or leg, usually developing after an injury or surgery, characterized by prolonged severe pain.
complex regional pain syndrome.advice: Seek early treatment. Physical therapy, nerve blocks, and medication can help manage symptoms.

spondylosis.description: Age-related wear and tear affecting the spinal discs and joints. Also known as spinal osteoarthritis, it commonly affects the neck and lower back.
spondylosis.advice: Maintain good posture, exercise regularly, use hot/cold therapy, and consult a doctor for pain management.

vaginal cyst.description: Small fluid-filled sacs that develop on or under the vaginal lining. Most are benign and may not cause symptoms.
vaginal cyst.advice: Most cysts resolve on their own. Warm baths can help. See a gynecologist if the cyst is painful or infected.

conjunctivitis due to allergy.description: Inflammation of the conjunctiva (eye membrane) triggered by allergens such as pollen, dust mites, or pet dander.
conjunctivitis due to allergy.advice: Avoid allergens, use antihistamine eye drops, apply cool compresses, and don't rub your eyes.

peripheral nerve disorder.description: Damage to the peripheral nerves causing weakness, numbness, and pain, usually in the hands and feet. Can result from diabetes, infections, or injuries.
peripheral nerve disorder.advice: Manage underlying conditions (e.g. diabetes), take prescribed medications, and consider physical therapy.

esophagitis.description: Inflammation of the esophagus that can cause pain, difficulty swallowing, and chest pain. Often caused by acid reflux or infection.
esophagitis.advice: Avoid spicy/acidic foods, eat smaller meals, don't lie down after eating, and see a gastroenterologist.

hypoglycemia.description: Abnormally low blood sugar levels, most common in people with diabetes. Can cause confusion, shakiness, and in severe cases, loss of consciousness.
hypoglycemia.advice: Immediately consume fast-acting sugar (juice, candy). If symptoms are severe, seek emergency care.

diverticulitis.description: Inflammation or infection of small pouches (diverticula) that form along the walls of the intestines, most commonly in the colon.
diverticulitis.advice: Liquid diet during flare-ups, antibiotics as prescribed, high-fiber diet for prevention. See a doctor promptly.

gastrointestinal hemorrhage.description: Bleeding anywhere in the gastrointestinal tract from the esophagus to the rectum. Can range from mild to life-threatening.
gastrointestinal hemorrhage.advice: Seek emergency medical care immediately. Do not eat or drink until evaluated by a doctor.

acute bronchitis.description: Short-term inflammation of the bronchial tubes, usually caused by viral infections. Often follows a cold or flu.
acute bronchitis.advice: Rest, stay hydrated, use a humidifier, and take OTC cough medicine. See a doctor if symptoms last over 3 weeks.

pneumonia.description: Infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. Can be caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi and ranges from mild to life-threatening.
pneumonia.advice: See a doctor immediately. Antibiotics are needed for bacterial pneumonia. Rest and stay hydrated.

infectious gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines caused by viral, bacterial, or parasitic infection. Commonly known as stomach flu.
infectious gastroenteritis.advice: Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions. Eat bland foods. Seek medical care if symptoms are severe.

spontaneous abortion.description: Natural loss of a pregnancy before the 20th week. Also known as miscarriage. Affects about 10-20% of known pregnancies.
spontaneous abortion.advice: Seek immediate medical attention. Emotional support is important. Follow-up care with an OB/GYN is essential.

sprain or strain.description: Sprains are stretched or torn ligaments, while strains affect muscles or tendons. Common in ankles, knees, and wrists.
sprain or strain.advice: Follow RICE protocol: Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation. See a doctor if you cannot bear weight.

fungal infection of the hair.description: Fungal infection affecting the scalp and hair shafts, commonly known as tinea capitis. Most common in children.
fungal infection of the hair.advice: Use antifungal shampoo and oral antifungals as prescribed. Avoid sharing combs, hats, and towels.

gout.description: A form of inflammatory arthritis caused by excess uric acid crystallizing in joints, typically the big toe. Causes sudden, severe pain.
gout.advice: Avoid high-purine foods (red meat, alcohol). Take anti-inflammatory medications. Stay hydrated.

arthritis of the hip.description: Degenerative joint disease affecting the hip joint, causing the cartilage to wear down. Most common in older adults.
arthritis of the hip.advice: Low-impact exercise, weight management, physical therapy, and pain medications. Surgery may be needed.

strep throat.description: Bacterial infection of the throat and tonsils caused by Group A Streptococcus. Highly contagious and common in children.
strep throat.advice: See a doctor for a rapid strep test. Antibiotics are essential to prevent complications like rheumatic fever.

liver disease.description: Any condition that damages the liver and affects its function, including hepatitis, cirrhosis, and fatty liver disease.
liver disease.advice: Avoid alcohol, maintain a healthy diet, get vaccinated for hepatitis, and see a specialist regularly.

bursitis.description: Inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs (bursae) that cushion bones, tendons, and muscles near joints. Common in shoulders, elbows, and hips.
bursitis.advice: Rest the affected joint, apply ice, take anti-inflammatory medications. Physical therapy may help.

acute pancreatitis.description: Sudden inflammation of the pancreas that can be mild or life-threatening. Often caused by gallstones or heavy alcohol use.
acute pancreatitis.advice: Seek emergency medical care. Treatment requires hospitalization with IV fluids and pain management.

spinal stenosis.description: Narrowing of the spaces within the spine, putting pressure on nerves. Most common in the lower back and neck.
spinal stenosis.advice: Physical therapy, pain medications, and steroid injections. Surgery may be needed in severe cases.

eczema.description: A chronic skin condition causing red, itchy, inflamed patches. Also known as atopic dermatitis. Often starts in childhood.
eczema.advice: Moisturize regularly, avoid triggers, use prescribed topical steroids, and wear soft fabrics.

benign prostatic hyperplasia (bph).description: Non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland, common in older men. Causes urinary problems by pressing on the urethra.
benign prostatic hyperplasia (bph).advice: Limit caffeine and alcohol, avoid fluids before bed. Medications or surgery may be needed.

noninfectious gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines not caused by infection, often triggered by food sensitivities, medications, or toxins.
noninfectious gastroenteritis.advice: Identify and avoid triggers. Stay hydrated, eat bland foods, and consult a doctor if symptoms persist.

cholecystitis.description: Inflammation of the gallbladder, usually caused by gallstones blocking the bile duct. Can become a medical emergency.
cholecystitis.advice: Seek medical attention promptly. Low-fat diet may help. Surgical removal of the gallbladder is often needed.

acute bronchiolitis.description: Infection of the small airways (bronchioles) in the lungs, most common in infants and young children. Usually caused by RSV.
acute bronchiolitis.advice: Keep the child hydrated, use a humidifier, monitor breathing. Seek emergency care if breathing is labored.

anxiety.description: A mental health condition characterized by persistent, excessive worry and fear about everyday situations. Can be debilitating.
anxiety.advice: Practice relaxation techniques, exercise regularly, limit caffeine. Consider therapy (CBT) and medication.

obstructive sleep apnea (osa).description: A sleep disorder where breathing repeatedly stops and starts during sleep due to throat muscles relaxing and blocking the airway.
obstructive sleep apnea (osa).advice: Use a CPAP machine, maintain healthy weight, avoid alcohol before bed, and sleep on your side.

concussion.description: A mild traumatic brain injury caused by a bump, blow, or jolt to the head. Temporarily affects brain function.
concussion.advice: Rest physically and mentally. Avoid screens. Seek emergency care if symptoms worsen or there's loss of consciousness.

dental caries.description: Tooth decay caused by bacteria producing acids that destroy tooth enamel. One of the most common chronic diseases worldwide.
dental caries.advice: Brush twice daily, floss, limit sugary foods. See a dentist for fillings or other treatments.

pyogenic skin infection.description: Bacterial skin infection producing pus, including conditions like impetigo, cellulitis, and abscesses. Often caused by Staphylococcus or Streptococcus.
pyogenic skin infection.advice: Keep the area clean, apply prescribed antibiotics. See a doctor for drainage if an abscess forms.

depression.description: A mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and loss of interest. Affects how you think, feel, and handle daily activities.
depression.advice: Seek professional help. Therapy (CBT), medication, exercise, and social support are all effective treatments.

acute sinusitis.description: Short-term inflammation of the sinuses, usually following a cold. Causes mucus buildup and facial pressure.
acute sinusitis.advice: Use saline nasal spray, steam inhalation, stay hydrated. Antibiotics may be needed for bacterial sinusitis.

herniated disk.description: A condition where the soft center of a spinal disc pushes through a crack in the tougher exterior, irritating nearby nerves.
herniated disk.advice: Physical therapy, pain medications, and activity modification. Surgery may be needed if conservative treatment fails.

chronic constipation.description: Persistent difficulty with bowel movements, defined as fewer than three stools per week for several weeks.
chronic constipation.advice: Increase fiber intake, drink more water, exercise regularly. Consider OTC laxatives or consult a doctor.

angina.description: Chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart muscle. A symptom of coronary artery disease and a warning sign of heart attack.
angina.advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect angina. Stop activity, rest, and call emergency services.

multiple sclerosis.description: A chronic autoimmune disease where the immune system attacks the protective covering of nerves, disrupting brain-body communication.
multiple sclerosis.advice: Early diagnosis is crucial. Disease-modifying therapies can slow progression. Work closely with a neurologist.

conjunctivitis.description: Inflammation or infection of the transparent membrane (conjunctiva) lining the eyelid and eyeball. Also known as pink eye.
conjunctivitis.advice: Avoid touching eyes, wash hands frequently. Bacterial conjunctivitis needs antibiotic drops.

psoriasis.description: A chronic autoimmune condition that causes rapid skin cell buildup, resulting in scaling on the skin's surface. Patches are itchy and sometimes painful.
psoriasis.advice: Moisturize regularly, use prescribed topical treatments, avoid triggers like stress and cold weather.

sepsis.description: A life-threatening condition where the body's response to infection damages its own tissues and organs. Requires immediate medical treatment.
sepsis.advice: SEEK EMERGENCY CARE IMMEDIATELY. Sepsis is a medical emergency. Every minute counts for survival.

panic disorder.description: An anxiety disorder characterized by unexpected, recurring panic attacks — sudden periods of intense fear with physical symptoms.
panic disorder.advice: Breathing exercises and grounding techniques during attacks. Long-term: therapy (CBT) and possibly medication.

diabetes.description: A metabolic disease causing high blood sugar levels. Type 1 is autoimmune; Type 2 is related to lifestyle and insulin resistance.
diabetes.advice: Monitor blood sugar regularly, maintain healthy diet and exercise, take medication as prescribed.

asthma.description: A chronic respiratory condition where airways narrow, swell, and produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.
asthma.advice: Use inhalers as prescribed, avoid triggers, have an asthma action plan, and seek emergency care for severe attacks.

hypertension.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure that increases risk of heart disease, stroke, and organ damage. Often called the 'silent killer'.
hypertension.advice: Reduce salt intake, exercise regularly, manage stress, limit alcohol, and take prescribed medications.

urinary tract infection.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system — kidneys, ureters, bladder, or urethra. Most common in the bladder and urethra.
urinary tract infection.advice: Drink plenty of water, take prescribed antibiotics, and urinate frequently. See a doctor if symptoms worsen.
